content:
Practical guidance for “Material salvage sorting yard” within “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”. It supports clear responsibilities, verified information, visible records, fair priorities, stop conditions, and a workable handover, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Material salvage sorting yard” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Stop condition: Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help. Apply this boundary to “Material salvage sorting yard” in “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience”.

Reduced fallback: If “Material salvage sorting yard” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a smaller documented task with one owner, one deadline, and one independent check.

Define the essential function, available energy, rated limits, hazards, and a manual fallback before connecting equipment. Record the result under “Rebuilding · Long-term · Resilience” before continuing “Material salvage sorting yard”.
